policy: Runtime link entries are plans, not execution. Do not write to target paths until the source primitive is promoted, validation passes, collisions are reviewed, and the user explicitly approves activation.

entries.name: codex-app-marketplace

reviewFindings.summary: Marketplace import is virtual and does not write to local runtime paths by itself.
evidence: targetPath uses the codex:// marketplace import URL.
sourcePath resolves to marketplace.json in the intelligence repository.

entries.notes: Primary activation path for referential plugins. User action still controls installation from the Codex app.

entries.name: codex-skill-children

reviewFindings.summary: ~/.codex/skills resolves through the Apollo skill home, and the current eligible intelligence skill children have no direct target collisions.
evidence: Existing canonical Apollo-backed skill links: define-goal and kotlin-standards.
Legacy Apollo aliases doc-coauthoring, kotlin-gradle-loop, segment-codebase-agents-md, and yeet already resolve to canonical intelligence skills under renamed local skill directories.
Current preflight reports no direct eligible SKILL child collisions; approval will still mutate /Users/amichne/code/apollo/skills by adding only missing symlinks.

entries.notes: Ready for explicit approval to add missing canonical intelligence skill children into the Apollo-backed Codex skill home as symlinks; approval remains required because the target is ~/.codex/skills -> /Users/amichne/code/apollo/skills.

entries.name: agent-skill-children

reviewFindings.summary: ~/.agents/skills has no direct collisions for eligible intelligence skill children after the targeted trims.
evidence: Preflight filters SKILL runtime child links to directories with SKILL.md, so skills/AGENTS.md is not part of the activation surface.
Current eligible intelligence skill children already active in ~/.agents/skills: kotlin-standards, manage-json-schemas, tdd.
The remaining eligible intelligence skill children are missing in ~/.agents/skills and would be added as symlinks only after approval.
Existing external ~/.agents/skills children such as .system, .DS_Store, gh-cli, gh-fix-ci, skill-creator, and manage-json-schemas.zip are not activation targets.

entries.notes: Ready for explicit approval to add missing intelligence skill children as symlinks while leaving external runtime skills and non-skill artifacts untouched.

entries.name: claude-agent-children

reviewFindings.summary: The Claude agent target exists and has no direct children.

entries.notes: Future activation still requires explicit approval before linking agent profiles.

entries.name: codex-hook-adapters

reviewFindings.summary: The Codex hook target is currently absent, so activation can create it after approval.
evidence: Preflight classifies a missing target path as READY_FOR_APPROVAL only when the runtime link operation is WAITING_APPROVAL.
Activation steps include mkdir -p /Users/amichne/.codex/hooks before linking children.

entries.notes: The approval packet will create the target directory before linking provider adapter configs. Keep provider-neutral hook metadata in this repository.
